text:
If you spent yesterday hiding from the smoke, today's the payoff: sunshine building through the morning and a high near 77, with that lingering smoke haze clearing out as the day goes on. The air's still Moderate early, so anyone sensitive might ease into outdoor plans rather than front-load them before 9. Wind's the real story out on the broad waters, a Lake Wind Advisory is up with northwest gusts into the 15-25 knot range and waves 1 to 3 feet, so if you're sailing or paddling today, plan around the chop rather than fighting it. Water's a pleasant 71 for anyone brave enough to jump in between gusts.

Monday is the pick of the two, sunny, calmer, a high near 83 with the lake laying down to a knot or less by afternoon, southwest winds easing through the day. Enjoy both, because the forecasters are already flagging showers moving in Tuesday into Wednesday. Best bet: get your lake time in Monday afternoon, winds should be light and manageable by early afternoon on through evening.
packet:
OBSERVED (2026-07-19T10:35:00+00:00): Mostly Cloudy, 63F feels 63F, humidity 68%, wind WNW 8 mph
NWS FORECAST:
  Today: Patchy smoke before 9am. Sunny, with a high near 77. Northwest wind around 14 mph.
  Tonight: Mostly clear, with a low around 57. West wind 5 to 13 mph.
  Monday: Sunny, with a high near 83. Southwest wind around 6 mph.
  Monday Night: Mostly cloudy, with a low around 64. South wind 3 to 13 mph.
AFD KEY MESSAGES (forecaster's reasoning, issued 2026-07-19T07:01:00+00:00):
  1. Dry through Monday then showers Tuesday into Wednesday.
  2. Near normal temperatures expected for the second half of the week into the weekend. No significant weather anticipated at this time.
AFD WHAT CHANGED: As of 255 AM EDT Sunday... No significant changes.
LAKE (broad waters):
  ..A Lake Wind Advisory Is In Effect: 
  Today: Northwest winds 15 to 25 knots. Sunny. Patchy smoke in the morning. Waves 1 to 3 feet.
  Tonight: Northwest winds 15 to 20 knots, becoming west 5 to 15 knots. Clear. Waves 1 to 2 feet.
  Monday: Southwest winds 10 knots or less, becoming south. Mostly sunny. Waves 1 foot or less.
LAKE GAGE: water 71F, level 96.01 ft (normal)
MODEL SPREAD (where forecasts diverge):
  2026-07-19: highs [GFS 73, Euro 73, ICON 73] spread 0F; precip chance [GFS 34%, Euro 72%, ICON 70%]
  2026-07-20: highs [GFS 82, Euro 78, ICON 78] spread 4F; precip chance [GFS 1%, Euro 8%, ICON 0%]
  2026-07-21: highs [GFS 76, Euro 76, ICON 75] spread 1F; precip chance [GFS 61%, Euro 80%, ICON 38%]
AIR: AQI 52 Moderate (PM2.5) — On Sunday, July 19th, expect moderate air quality across Vermont as some remnant wildfire smoke remains in the area.  Levels will tend to drop over the course of the day.
SUN: rise 2026-07-19T05:26-04:00, set 2026-07-19T20:31-04:00, UV max 7.3
WEATHER UNDERGROUND / WEATHER.COM:
  high 76, low 58: Times of sun and clouds. Highs in the mid 70s and lows in the upper 50s.
  high 82, low 66: Mostly sunny. Highs in the low 80s and lows in the mid 60s.
WCAX METEOROLOGIST: BURLINGTON, Vt. (WCAX) -Happy Sunday! The second half of the weekend is looking a lot nicer than the first. Smoke and poor air quality made their departure through the night last night, and we’ll be left with more and more sunshine emerging into this afternoon. Highs for the day will climb back into the 70s. We do hang onto a bit of a breeze with winds out of the north and west at times gusting upwards of 25-30 MPH, which could possibly add a little more chop to Lake Champlain if you do have any plans out on the lake. Clear skies last into tonight, as lows fall into the 50s.
